step.title: Load Bundle
step.goal: Read the bundle into working memory. The Design Summary (written by ticket-design) is the primary input — its artifact-chain shape informs the ticket's structure.

substep.title: Locate the Bundle
substep.ask:
Which ticket bundle should I structure?
Paste the bundle directory path or the ticket file path. The bundle should already have both a Readiness Summary section (written by ticket-grill) AND a Design Summary section (written by ticket-design) in the ticket body.
substep.action: Validate the path. Store bundle paths in session (incl. {{session.worklog_path}}). Verify the WORKLOG contains BOTH "## Q↔R Readiness Summary" AND "## Design Summary". If either is absent, halt — the user must run the prior phase first (ticket-grill or ticket-design).

substep.title: Read Bundle Files
substep.action:
Read the ticket, research, and questions files completely. Extract: - Primary tag and slug - Design Summary content (artifact chain, decision points, risk areas, locked DRs) - Any prior structure attempts (a session resuming ticket-structure may have a partial outline already present) - Linked DRs (their decisions may shape sections — e.g., a DR about a multi-tenancy choice means the structure outline should have a "Data Isolation" section even if the recipe doesn't mandate it)
Store in session: primary_tag, slug, design_summary, artifact_chain, locked_drs, prior_structure_attempt.

substep.title: Display Load Summary

section.title: What the Design Summary tells us about structure
section.description: The Design Summary's artifact chain hints at section grouping. For a feature ticket with chain [DR → Brief update → ADR → Architecture → Spec → Implementation], the ticket's structure will likely include sections for the product decision (linking the DR), the architectural decision (linking the ADR), the behavioral contract (referenced from the future Spec), and the implementation scope. Each chain item maps loosely to one or more sections; the recipe formalizes the mapping per tag.

section.title: DR-driven section additions
section.description:
Each linked DR is a load-bearing decision. The structure should SURFACE these decisions in dedicated sections (or sub-sections), not bury them in prose. Examples: - DR on multi-tenancy → "Data Isolation Strategy" section - DR on vendor choice → "Vendor Selection" section - DR on scope boundary → "What This Does NOT Cover" section
These additions may override recipe defaults; step 2 reconciles.
